text:
GYNECOLOGIC ONCOLOGY - CHEMOTHERAPY VISIT NOTE
Mid-Treatment Assessment: Paraplatin/Taxol Cycle 3 of 6

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR VISIT:
Margaret Delacroix is a 52-year-old postmenopausal woman with FIGO stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (germline BRCA1-positive, 5382insC), status post R0 primary debulking surgery followed by adjuvant Paraplatin/pacli chemotherapy. She presents today for mid-treatment assessment following completion of her third cycle of carbo/PTX.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Delacroix was diagnosed with advanced high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma approximately 5 months ago, with initial CA-125 of 1,847 U/mL and large-volume ascites on staging CT. She underwent uncomplicated primary debulking surgery approximately 4.5 months ago achieving complete gross resection (R0), with final surgical pathology confirming pT3c pN1 M0 disease (6 of 36 lymph nodes positive; omental cake resected en bloc; diaphragmatic peritoneal stripping performed). Post-operative CA-125 on day 21 declined to 342 U/mL.

She began adjuvant carbo AUC 6 / pacli 175 mg/m2 IV q21 days approximately 10 weeks ago. Cycle 1 was administered without significant incident. During cycle 2, she developed Grade 2 peripheral sensory neuropathy with bilateral hand and foot paresthesias (numbness and tingling at fingertips and toes, not interfering with ADLs), as well as complete scalp alopecia consistent with pacli and Grade 1 nausea adequately controlled with scheduled antiemetics. Dose reductions were deferred at the time of cycle 2 given the neuropathy was non-functional and the patient wished to continue full-dose therapy. She received cycle 3 at full dose (Paraplatin 640 mg, Taxol 315 mg based on BSA 1.80 m2) approximately 3 weeks ago and has now returned for mid-treatment assessment.

Since cycle 3, she reports the following:
- Peripheral neuropathy: persistent and somewhat bothersome, bilateral feet greater than hands. She describes a constant baseline numbness in both soles and all toes, with intermittent "electric-like" tingling. She reports mild difficulty with balance on uneven surfaces. She is not experiencing frank weakness, foot drop, or difficulty walking on flat ground. No impairment of fine motor tasks such as buttoning at this time.
- Fatigue: she rates fatigue as moderately limiting - she has been able to perform her light household tasks but has taken a leave of absence from work as a librarian since starting chemotherapy. She naps daily. This is consistent with CTCAE Grade 2 fatigue.
- Nausea: minimal, Grade 1, well-controlled with ondansetron PRN and scheduled dexamethasone. No vomiting. Appetite mildly decreased but maintaining weight.
- Alopecia: complete scalp hair loss. Eyebrow and eyelash thinning. She is using a wig and is adjusting emotionally.
- Bowel habits: some constipation requiring fiber supplementation and MiraLAX. No diarrhea. No blood in stool.
- Musculoskeletal: diffuse mild myalgia post-infusion, resolving by day 5 each cycle.
- Infectious symptoms: no fever, no chills, no cough, no dysuria, no neutropenic symptoms reported.
- No hospitalizations since starting chemotherapy.

She also asks about next steps after completing chemotherapy, including maintenance therapy. We have previously discussed nira as a maintenance option given her germline BRCA1 status and the expectation of completing chemotherapy with response. She has reviewed written materials provided at the last visit.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive: fatigue (Grade 2), peripheral neuropathy bilateral hands/feet (Grade 2, feet > hands), alopecia (complete), mild nausea (Grade 1), constipation, decreased appetite, myalgia post-infusion (resolving).
Negative: no fever, no night sweats, no dyspnea, no chest pain, no palpitations, no visual changes, no headache, no dizziness, no syncope, no edema, no abdominal pain, no new pelvic pain, no hematuria, no melena, no hematochezia, no oral mucositis, no rash, no jaundice. No symptoms of bowel obstruction.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
General: Well-appearing, pleasant woman in no acute distress. Wearing a wig. Mildly fatigued appearance. Alert and oriented.
Vitals: Temperature 36.9°C, BP 118/74 mmHg, HR 76 bpm, RR 14/min, SpO2 98% on room air. Weight 71.8 kg (stable; baseline 72 kg at start of chemotherapy). BMI 25.9 kg/m2.
HEENT: Alopecia, minimal residual eyebrow hair. Oropharynx without mucositis or thrush. No cervical lymphadenopathy.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs. No peripheral edema.
Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No crackles, no wheezing.
Abdomen: Well-healed midline laparotomy scar. Abdomen soft, non-tender, non-distended. No palpable organomegaly. No palpable mass. No ascites by clinical exam (abdomen flat, no shifting dullness). Bowel sounds present.
Pelvic: Not formally examined today; post-hysterectomy/BSO vault healing confirmed at prior visit, no current pelvic complaints.
Extremities: No edema. No calf tenderness.
Neurological: Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Strength 5/5 in all extremities. Sensation: decreased light touch and vibration in bilateral feet to mid-ankle level; bilateral fingertip sensation mildly diminished. Proprioception mildly reduced at bilateral great toes. No foot drop on heel walk today. Romberg test: mild sway but not positive fall. Gait: slightly cautious on tandem walking but functional.
Skin: No rash. Mild skin dryness.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS:
CBC (obtained today, prior to scheduled discussion):
  - WBC: 4.2 x10^3/uL
  - ANC: 2,080/uL (adequate)
  - Hemoglobin: 9.8 g/dL (Grade 1 anemia, stable from prior)
  - Hematocrit: 29.6%
  - Platelets: 138,000/uL (low-normal but Grade 0-1 range)
  ANC nadir post-cycle 3 (day 10 CBC per patient-reported values from outside lab): ANC 980/uL (Grade 2 neutropenia), recovered without intervention, no fever recorded.

Comprehensive metabolic panel:
  - Creatinine: 0.80 mg/dL; eGFR >60 mL/min/1.73m2 (CKD-EPI) - renal function preserved, appropriate for continued carbo at AUC 6
  - BUN: 12 mg/dL
  - Sodium: 138 mEq/L; Potassium: 3.9 mEq/L; Chloride: 103 mEq/L; Bicarbonate: 25 mEq/L
  - Glucose: 94 mg/dL
  - AST: 24 U/L; ALT: 31 U/L; Alkaline phosphatase: 68 U/L; Total bilirubin: 0.7 mg/dL (hepatic function normal)
  - Albumin: 3.6 g/dL
  - Magnesium: 1.8 mg/dL (low-normal; oral supplementation recommended)

Tumor markers:
  - CA-125: 78 U/mL (down from 342 U/mL post-operatively; not yet normalized to <35 U/mL but trending favorably in the context of 3 completed cycles)

---

IMAGING RESULTS:
Mid-treatment restaging CT Abdomen/Pelvis with contrast (performed prior to this visit, after completion of cycle 3): No ascites. No residual omental mass. No peritoneal implants visible on CT. No pelvic or para-aortic lymphadenopathy - previously enlarged lymph nodes now within normal limits. Liver surface and parenchyma unremarkable. No new findings. Post-surgical pelvic changes stable. Impression per radiology: no radiographically evident disease; response consistent with complete radiographic response to date.

This is highly encouraging in the context of initially bulky stage IIIC disease and is consistent with a biochemically and radiographically responding course. CA-125 trajectory likewise confirms objective response.

---

ASSESSMENT:
1. FIGO stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma, germline BRCA1-positive (5382insC), status post R0 primary debulking surgery, now mid-treatment on adjuvant Paraplatin/pacli cycle 3 of 6 -- RESPONDING. Radiographic complete response on mid-treatment CT. CA-125 78 U/mL, down from 342 U/mL post-operatively and 1,847 U/mL at diagnosis. Clinical course is favorable.

2. Chemotherapy-related peripheral sensory neuropathy, CTCAE Grade 2. Bilateral, feet greater than hands. Attributable to cumulative pacli (cumulative dose approximately 945 mg/m2 through 3 cycles). Stable compared with prior visit; not progressing acutely but patient notes ongoing functional impact with balance on uneven ground. Currently at a critical decision point -- will monitor closely at cycles 4 and 5 for progression to Grade 3.

3. Grade 1 anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL). Chemotherapy-related, stable, non-transfusion-requiring. No EPO/ESA initiated given stability and proximity to anticipated end of treatment.

4. Grade 2 fatigue, chemotherapy-related. Consistent with multi-agent platinum/taxane regimen in a heavily treated patient. Managed conservatively with activity pacing, rest, and supportive care.

5. Menopausal symptoms secondary to surgical menopause (post-BSO). Patient managing with non-hormonal interventions as discussed. Given BRCA1 status, hormone therapy is not being pursued at this time.

6. Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (5382insC). Genetic counseling completed. Cascade testing recommended for first-degree relatives. Patient's daughter has been referred.

---

PLAN:
1. Chemotherapy continuation: Proceed with carbo AUC 6 / Taxol 175 mg/m2 IV q21 days for cycles 4, 5, and 6 as planned. Current CBC supports proceeding. Renal function adequate. Hepatic function normal. No dose reduction warranted at this time.
   - Pre-medications: dexamethasone 20 mg IV, diphenhydramine 50 mg IV, famotidine 20 mg IV, ondansetron 8 mg IV.

2. Peripheral neuropathy monitoring: Will reassess neuropathy score at each subsequent cycle. If neuropathy progresses to Grade 3 (pain or functional limitation), will dose-reduce pacli to 135 mg/m2 (dose level -1) per institutional and package-insert guidelines. Patient instructed to contact office immediately if new weakness, foot drop, or significant functional decline develops. Physical therapy referral placed prophylactically for gait training and balance exercises.

3. Magnesium supplementation: Oral magnesium oxide 400 mg twice daily started for low-normal Mg 1.8 mg/dL (may reduce neuropathy progression per supporting data).

4. Fatigue management: Reviewed activity recommendations (gentle walking, aerobic activity as tolerated per NCCN fatigue guidelines). Continued leave of absence from work supported.

5. Anemia: Continue to monitor Hgb. Transfusion threshold set at Hgb <7.5 g/dL or symptomatic anemia (currently asymptomatic, Hgb 9.8). Iron studies ordered to rule out concurrent iron deficiency contributing to anemia.

6. Maintenance therapy planning: Discussed in detail with patient and husband. Given germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant, HRD-positive tumor (HRD score 56), and current trajectory toward complete response, patient is an excellent candidate for PARP inhibitor maintenance following completion of chemotherapy. Reviewed data from SOLO-1 trial (ola, first-line BRCA-mutant HRD-positive ovarian cancer) and PRIMA trial (Zejula, first-line maintenance regardless of HRD/BRCA status with enhanced benefit in BRCA-mutant/HRD-positive). Patient asked thoughtful questions and has reviewed written information. Will finalize maintenance decision at end-of-treatment visit. Patient is leaning toward Zejula for once-daily dosing convenience but has not yet decided.

7. Next visit: Cycle 4 chemotherapy administration in 3 weeks. Interim CBC check at day 10-14 post-cycle 3 (patient to have CBC done locally). Call office with any fever >38.0°C, bleeding, or new neurologic symptoms.

8. Psychosocial: Validated patient's experience of treatment burden (alopecia, neuropathy, fatigue, work leave). Oncology social worker notified. Support group for ovarian cancer survivors discussed.

Patient and husband were present for the full discussion, questions answered. Patient understands the plan and verbalized understanding. Return in 3 weeks for cycle 4.

Electronically signed,
Attending Gynecologic Oncologist
